## reference · ux placeholder text labels input pitfalls
* Avoid using placeholder text as a replacement for field labels, as it can cause usability issues.
* Placeholder text can strain users' short-term memory, especially for complex or rarely used forms.
* Without labels, users cannot easily check their work before submitting a form.
* Error messages can be confusing without visible labels or instructions.
* Placeholder text can be irritating for users navigating with the keyboard.
* Fields with placeholder text are less noticeable, which can cause users to overlook them.
* Users may mistake placeholder text for automatically filled-in data and skip the field.
* Occasionally, users have to manually delete placeholder text that doesn't disappear when typing.
* Using placeholder text in addition to labels can still cause issues, especially for users with visual or cognitive impairments.
* Placeholder text is generally bad for accessibility due to poor color contrast, inconsistent screen reader support, and increased burden on users with cognitive or motor impairments.
* Floating labels can mitigate some issues but still have limitations, such as fields with text being less noticeable and potential accessibility issues.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/form-design-placeholders/
